why: Protocol 775 Login clientbound minecraft:login_disconnect must dispatch from the official framed packet id to a Login disconnect reason decoder through Stevenarella packet_by_id. This is packet framing/dispatch/decode evidence for one official empty literal Component reason fixture only and is separate from UI disconnect handling, screen flow, authentication failure handling, Configuration entry, Play readiness, or client-load completion.
what: The Rust oracle test login_disconnect_clientbound_framed_dispatch_matches_official_oracle_answer panics while exercising packet::packet_by_id(775, State::Login, Direction::Clientbound, packet id 0, official body bytes) because v26_1_2.rs does not map Login clientbound packet id 0x00.

evidence.summary: Generated from official client.jar direct calls: Component.literal(String), ClientboundLoginDisconnectPacket(Component), ClientboundLoginDisconnectPacket.STREAM_CODEC, LoginProtocols.CLIENTBOUND.codec().encode/decode(ClientboundLoginDisconnectPacket), LoginProtocols.CLIENTBOUND_TEMPLATE.details().listPackets(...), ClientboundLoginDisconnectPacket.reason(), Component.getString(), and ClientLoginPacketListener extends ClientCookiePacketListener.
Manifest declares the exact Rust oracle test and framed_dispatch_decode comparison surface.
Rust test reads the manifest and answer, splits official framed VarInt packet id from body bytes, and calls packet::packet_by_id for Login clientbound login_disconnect dispatch/decode.
Current Protocol 775 Login clientbound mapping is empty and does not map official packet id 0x00 to login_disconnect.

observed_failure: bad packet id 0x0 in Clientbound Login; Stevenarella panicked while dispatching official Login clientbound login_disconnect packet id 0
